text.NRSV: After the death of Saul, when David had returned from defeating the Amalekites, David remained two days in Ziklag.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, after the death of Saul, when David had returned from the smiting of the Amalekites, and David had abode in Ziklag two days,

text.NRSV: On the third day, a man came from Saul’s camp, with his clothes torn and dirt on his head. When he came to David, he fell to the ground and did obeisance.
text.Rotherham: yea so it was, on the third day, that lo! a man came out of the camp, from Saul, with his clothes rent, and earth upon his head,—and so it was, when he came in unto David, that he fell to the earth, and did homage.

text.NRSV: David said to him, “Where have you come from?” He said to him, “I have escaped from the camp of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto him, From whence comest thou? And he said unto him, Out of the camp of Israel, am I escaped.

text.NRSV: David said to him, “How did things go? Tell me!” He answered, “The army fled from the battle, but also many of the army fell and died; and Saul and his son Jonathan also died.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto him—How turned out the matter? tell me, I pray thee. And he said—The people have fled from the battle, yea moreover, many, of the people have fallen, and died, Yea moreover, Saul, and Jonathan his son, are dead.

text.NRSV: Then David asked the young man who was reporting to him, “How do you know that Saul and his son Jonathan died?”
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto the young man who was telling him,—How knowest thou that Saul and Jonathan his son are dead?

text.NRSV: The young man reporting to him said, “I happened to be on Mount Gilboa; and there was Saul leaning on his spear, while the chariots and the horsemen drew close to him.
text.Rotherham: And the young man who was telling him said, It, so happened, that I was on Mount Gilboa, when lo! Saul, leaning upon his spear, and lo! the chariots and horsemen, hotly pursued him;

text.NRSV: When he looked behind him, he saw me, and called to me. I answered, ‘Here sir.’
text.Rotherham: so he turned behind him, and saw me,—and cried out unto me, and I said, Behold me!

text.NRSV: And he said to me, ‘Who are you?’ I answered him, ‘I am an Amalekite.’
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me, Who art thou? And I said unto him, An Amalekite, am I.

text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Come, stand over me and kill me; for convulsions have seized me, and yet my life still lingers.’
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me, I pray thee, take thy stand by me, and put me to death, for the cramp hath seized me,—even for as long as my life shall be in me.

text.NRSV: So I stood over him, and killed him, for I knew that he could not live after he had fallen. I took the crown that was on his head and the armlet that was on his arm, and I have brought them here to my lord.”
text.Rotherham: So I took my stand by him, and put him to death, for I perceived that he could not survive his fall,—so I took the crown that was on his head, and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them in unto my lord, here.

text.NRSV: Then David took hold of his clothes and tore them; and all the men who were with him did the same.
text.Rotherham: Then David took hold of his clothes, and rent them,—yea moreover, [so did] all the men who were with him.

text.NRSV: They mourned and wept, and fasted until evening for Saul and for his son Jonathan, and for the army of the Lord and for the house of Israel, because they had fallen by the sword.
text.Rotherham: And they lamented aloud, and wept, and fasted until the evening,—for Saul, and for Jonathan his son, and for the people of Yahweh, and for the house of Israel, because they had fallen by the sword.

text.NRSV: David said to the young man who had reported to him, “Where do you come from?” He answered, “I am the son of a resident alien, an Amalekite.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto the young man who was telling him, Whence art thou? And he said, Son of a sojourner—an Amalekite, am I.

text.NRSV: David said to him, “Were you not afraid to lift your hand to destroy the Lord’s anointed?”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto him,—How wast thou not afraid to thrust forth thy hand, to destroy the Anointed of Yahweh?

text.NRSV: Then David called one of the young men and said, “Come here and strike him down.” So he struck him down and he died.
text.Rotherham: So David called one of the young men, and said,—Go near—fall upon him. And he smote him, that he died.

text.NRSV: David said to him, “Your blood be on your head; for your own mouth has testified against you, saying, ‘I have killed the Lord’s anointed.’”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto him, Thy blood, be upon thine own head,—for, thine own mouth, hath testified against thee, saying, I myself, put to death the Anointed of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: David intoned this lamentation over Saul and his son Jonathan.
text.Rotherham: Then chanted David this dirge,—over Saul and over Jonathan his son;

text.NRSV: (He ordered that The Song of the Bow be taught to the people of Judah; it is written in the Book of Jashar.) He said:
text.Rotherham: and he thought to teach the sons of Judah [the song of] the Bow,—lo! it is written in the Book of the Upright:—

text.NRSV: Your glory, O Israel, lies slain upon your high places! How the mighty have fallen!
text.Rotherham: The beauty of Israel! on thy high plumes—slain! How have fallen—the mighty!

text.NRSV: Tell it not in Gath, proclaim it not in the streets of Ashkelon; or the daughters of the Philistines will rejoice, the daughters of the uncircumcised will exult.
text.Rotherham: Do not tell it in Gath, Do not publish it in the streets of Ashkelon,—lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the Uncircumcised triumph.

text.NRSV: You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain upon you, nor bounteous fields! For there the shield of the mighty was defiled, the shield of Saul, anointed with oil no more.
text.Rotherham: Ye mountains in Gilboa! Be there neither dew nor rain upon you, nor fields of offerings,—for, there, were cast away, the shields of the mighty, The shield of Saul, unanointed with oil.

text.NRSV: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan did not turn back, nor the sword of Saul return empty.
text.Rotherham: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, the bow of Jonathan ne’er drew back,—and, the sword of Saul, ne’er returned, empty.

text.NRSV: Saul and Jonathan, beloved and lovely! In life and in death they were not divided; they were swifter than eagles, they were stronger than lions.
text.Rotherham: Saul and Jonathan, delightfully loving in their lives, even, in their death, were not divided,—Beyond eagles, were they swift, beyond lions, were they strong!

text.NRSV: O daughters of Israel, weep over Saul, who clothed you with crimson, in luxury, who put ornaments of gold on your apparel.
text.Rotherham: Ye daughters of Israel! For Saul, weep ye,—who clothed you in crimson, [decked] with lovely things, who hung ornaments of gold on your apparel!

text.NRSV: How the mighty have fallen in the midst of the battle! Jonathan lies slain upon your high places.
text.Rotherham: How have fallen the mighty, in the midst of the battle! Jonathan, on thy high places, slain!

text.NRSV: I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan; greatly beloved were you to me; your love to me was wonderful, passing the love of women.
text.Rotherham: I am distressed for thee, my brother, Jonathan! Delightful to me, exceedingly,—Wonderful, was thy love to me, passing the love of women.

text.NRSV: How the mighty have fallen, and the weapons of war perished!
text.Rotherham: How have fallen the mighty, and perished the weapons of war!

text.NRSV: After this David inquired of the Lord, “Shall I go up into any of the cities of Judah?” The Lord said to him, “Go up.” David said, “To which shall I go up?” He said, “To Hebron.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that David enquired of Yahweh, saying—Shall I go up into one of the cities of Judah? And Yahweh said unto him, Go up. And David said—Whither shall I go up? And he said—Unto Hebron.

text.NRSV: So David went up there, along with his two wives, Ahinoam of Jezreel, and Abigail the widow of Nabal of Carmel.
text.Rotherham: So David went up thither, and his two wives also,—Ahinoam, the Jezreelitess, and Abigail, wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

text.NRSV: David brought up the men who were with him, every one with his household; and they settled in the towns of Hebron.
text.Rotherham: His men also who were with him, did David bring up, every man with his household,—and they dwelt in the cities of Hebron.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Judah came, and there they anointed David king over the house of Judah. When David was told that the people of Jabesh-gilead were the ones who had buried Saul,
text.Rotherham: Then came the men of Judah, and anointed David there, to be king over the house of Judah. And they told David, saying, Men of Jabesh-gilead, were they who buried Saul.

text.NRSV: David sent messengers to the people of Jabesh-gilead, and said to them, “May you be blessed by the Lord, because you showed this loyalty to Saul your lord, and buried him!
text.Rotherham: So David sent messengers unto the men of Jabesh-gilead,—and said unto them—Blessed, be ye of Yahweh, that ye did this lovingkindness unto your lord, unto Saul, and buried him.

text.NRSV: Now may the Lord show steadfast love and faithfulness to you! And I too will reward you because you have done this thing.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, Yahweh requite you with lovingkindness and truth,—moreover also, I, will requite you, for this noble deed, in that ye did this thing.

text.NRSV: Therefore let your hands be strong, and be valiant; for Saul your lord is dead, and the house of Judah has anointed me king over them.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let your hands be made firm, and become ye sons of valour, for your lord Saul is dead,—and, me, moreover have the house of Judah anointed, to be king over them.

text.NRSV: But Abner son of Ner, commander of Saul’s army, had taken Ishbaal son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim.
text.Rotherham: But, Abner son of Ner, prince of the host that pertained unto Saul, took Ish-bosheth, son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim;

text.NRSV: He made him king over Gilead, the Ashurites, Jezreel, Ephraim, Benjamin, and over all Israel.
text.Rotherham: and made him king for Gilead, and for the Ashurites, and for Jezreel,—and over Ephraim, and over Benjamin, and over, all Israel.

text.NRSV: Ishbaal, Saul’s son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.
text.Rotherham: Forty years old, was Ish-bosheth son of Saul, when he began to reign over Israel, and, two years, reigned he,—but, the house of Judah, followed David.

text.NRSV: The time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, the number of the days that David was king in Hebron, over the house of Judah, was seven years and six months.

text.NRSV: Abner son of Ner, and the servants of Ishbaal son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.
text.Rotherham: And Abner son of Ner, and the servants of Ish-bosheth, son of Saul, came out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah, and the servants of David, went out and met them at the pool of Gibeon. One group sat on one side of the pool, the other group on the other side of the pool.
text.Rotherham: And, Joab son of Zeruiah, and the servants of David, went out from Hebron and met, by the pool of Gibeon, together, —and they sat down, these, by the pool, on the one side, and, those, by the pool, on the other side.

text.NRSV: Abner said to Joab, “Let the young men come forward and have a contest before us.” Joab said, “Let them come forward.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Abner unto Joab, Let the young men arise, I pray thee, and make sport before us. And Joab said—Let them arise.

text.NRSV: So they came forward and were counted as they passed by, twelve for Benjamin and Ishbaal son of Saul, and twelve of the servants of David.
text.Rotherham: Then there arose, and went over by number,—twelve of Benjamin, pertaining to Ish-bosheth, son of Saul, and twelve, of the servants of David.

text.NRSV: Each grasped his opponent by the head, and thrust his sword in his opponent’s side; so they fell down together. Therefore that place was called Helkath-hazzurim, which is at Gibeon.
text.Rotherham: And they caught every one his fellow by the head, with his sword in his fellow’s side, so they fell, together,—and that place was called Helkath-hazzadim ["Field of Plotters"], which is in Gideon.

text.NRSV: The battle was very fierce that day; and Abner and the men of Israel were beaten by the servants of David.
text.Rotherham: So the battle became exceeding obstinate, on that day,—and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated before the servants of David.

text.NRSV: The three sons of Zeruiah were there, Joab, Abishai, and Asahel. Now Asahel was as swift of foot as a wild gazelle.
text.Rotherham: Now there were there, three sons of Zeruiah, Joab, and Abishai, and Asahel,—and, Asahel, was light of foot as a wild gazelle.

text.NRSV: Asahel pursued Abner, turning neither to the right nor to the left as he followed him.
text.Rotherham: And Asahel pursued Abner,—and turned not, in going, to the right hand or to the left, from pursuing Abner.

text.NRSV: Then Abner looked back and said, “Is it you, Asahel?” He answered, “Yes.”
text.Rotherham: Then Abner looked behind him, and said—Art thou Asahel? And he said—I am.

text.NRSV: Abner said to him, “Turn to your right or to your left, and seize one of the young men, and take his spoil.” But Asahel would not turn away from following him.
text.Rotherham: Then Abner said to him—Turn thee aside, to thy right hand, or to thy left, and lay thee hold on one of the young men, and take thee his armour. But Asahel would not turn aside from pursuing him.

text.NRSV: Abner said again to Asahel, “Turn away from following me; why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I show my face to your brother Joab?”
text.Rotherham: And Abner said, yet again, unto Asahel, Turn thee aside from pursuing me,—wherefore should I smite thee to the earth? how then should I lift up my face unto Joab, thy brother?

text.NRSV: But he refused to turn away. So Abner struck him in the stomach with the butt of his spear, so that the spear came out at his back. He fell there, and died where he lay. And all who came to the place where Asahel had fallen and died stood still.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit he refused to turn aside, wherefore Abner smote him with the hinder end of the spear, in the belly, that the spear came out behind him, and he fell there, and died on the spot,—and so it was, that, as many as came up to the place where Asahel fell and died, stood still.

text.NRSV: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner; and as the sun was going down they came to the hill of Ammah, which lies before Giah on the way to the wilderness of Gibeon.
text.Rotherham: Then Joab and Abishai pursued Abner,—but, the sun, had gone in by the time, they, had come as far as the hill of Ammah, that overlooketh Giah, by way of the wilderness of Gibeon.

text.NRSV: The Benjaminites gathered together behind Abner and became one troop, and took their stand on the top of a hill.
text.Rotherham: And, when the sons of Benjamin had gathered themselves together after Abner, and become one band,—and had taken their stand on the top of a certain hill,

text.NRSV: Then Abner called to Joab, “Is the sword to keep devouring forever? Do you not know that the end will be bitter? How long will it be before you order your people to turn from the pursuit of their kinsmen?”
text.Rotherham: then Abner cried aloud unto Joab, and said—To the uttermost, must the sword devour? Dost thou not well know that, bitter, shall it be, in the latter end? How long, then, wilt thou not bid the people turn back from pursuing their own brethren?

text.NRSV: Joab said, “As God lives, if you had not spoken, the people would have continued to pursue their kinsmen until the morning.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joab—As God liveth, surely, if thou hadst not spoken, then in the morning, the people would of themselves have gone up, every man from pursuing his brother.

text.NRSV: Joab sounded the trumpet and all the people stopped; they no longer pursued Israel or engaged in battle any further.
text.Rotherham: So Joab blew with the horn, and all the people stood still, and they neither pursued Israel further,—nor fought any more.

text.NRSV: Abner and his men traveled all that night through the Arabah; they crossed the Jordan, and, marching the whole forenoon, they came to Mahanaim.
text.Rotherham: And, Abner and his men, journeyed through the waste plain all that night,—and crossed the Jordan, and went through all the Bithron, and entered Mahanaim.

text.NRSV: Joab returned from the pursuit of Abner; and when he had gathered all the people together, there were missing of David’s servants nineteen men besides Asahel.
text.Rotherham: And, Joab, returned from pursuing Abner, and, when he had gathered together all the people, there were missing of the servants of David—nineteen men, and Asahel;

text.NRSV: But the servants of David had killed of Benjamin three hundred sixty of Abner’s men.
text.Rotherham: but, the servants of David, had smitten of Benjamin, of the men of Abner,—[so that] three hundred and sixty men, had died.

text.NRSV: They took up Asahel and buried him in the tomb of his father, which was at Bethlehem. Joab and his men marched all night, and the day broke upon them at Hebron.
text.Rotherham: And they carried Asahel, and buried him in the grave of his father, which was in Bethlehem,—and Joab and his men journeyed all the night, and had daylight in Hebron.

text.NRSV: There was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David; David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul became weaker and weaker.
text.Rotherham: And the war between the house of Saul and the house of David was prolonged,—and, David, waxed stronger and stronger, and, the house of Saul, became weaker and weaker.

text.NRSV: Sons were born to David at Hebron: his firstborn was Amnon, of Ahinoam of Jezreel;
text.Rotherham: And there were born unto David, sons, in Hebron,—and it came to press that—His firstborn was Amnon, by Ahinoam, the Jezreelitess;

text.NRSV: his second, Chileab, of Abigail the widow of Nabal of Carmel; the third, Absalom son of Maacah, daughter of King Talmai of Geshur;
text.Rotherham: And his second Chileab, by Abigail, wife of Nabal the Carmelite, And the third Absolom, son of Maachah, daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur;

text.NRSV: the fourth, Adonijah son of Haggith; the fifth, Shephatiah son of Abital;
text.Rotherham: And the fourth Adonijah, son of Higgith,—And the fifth Shephatiah, son of Abital;

text.NRSV: and the sixth, Ithream, of David’s wife Eglah. These were born to David in Hebron.
text.Rotherham: And the sixth Ithream, by Eglah wife of David. These, were born unto David, in Hebron.

text.NRSV: While there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, Abner was making himself strong in the house of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while the war continued between the house of Saul and the house of David, that, Abner, shewed himself courageous for the house of Saul.

text.NRSV: Now Saul had a concubine whose name was Rizpah daughter of Aiah. And Ishbaal said to Abner, “Why have you gone in to my father’s concubine?”
text.Rotherham: Now, Saul, had a concubine, whose name, was Rizpah daughter of Aiah,—and [Ish-bosheth] said unto Abner, Why, wentest thou in, unto my father’s concubine?

text.NRSV: The words of Ishbaal made Abner very angry; he said, “Am I a dog’s head for Judah? Today I keep showing loyalty to the house of your father Saul, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not given you into the hand of David; and yet you charge me now with a crime concerning this woman.
text.Rotherham: And it provoked Abner greatly, on account of the words of Ish-bosheth, and he said—Am I, the head of the dogs, that pertain unto Judah? To-day, would I deal in lovingkindness with the house of Saul thy father, towards his brethren, and towards his friends, and have not delivered thee into the hand of David,—and yet thou hast charged upon me the transgression of the woman, today.

text.NRSV: So may God do to Abner and so may he add to it! For just what the Lord has sworn to David, that will I accomplish for him,
text.Rotherham: So, may God do unto Abner, and, so, may he add to him,—if, as Yahweh hath sworn unto David, even so, I deal not with him;

text.NRSV: to transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul, and set up the throne of David over Israel and over Judah, from Dan to Beer-sheba.”
text.Rotherham: by turning over the kingdom from the house of Saul,—and establishing the throne of David, over Israel and over Judah, from Dan, even unto Beer-sheba.

text.NRSV: And Ishbaal could not answer Abner another word, because he feared him.
text.Rotherham: And he could no further answer Abner, a word,—because he feared him.

text.NRSV: Abner sent messengers to David at Hebron, saying, “To whom does the land belong? Make your covenant with me, and I will give you my support to bring all Israel over to you.”
text.Rotherham: So Abner sent messengers unto David, on the spot, saying—Whose, is the land? Saying [also] —Solemnise thy covenant with me; and lo! my hand, shall be with thee, to bring round unto thee, all Israel.

text.NRSV: He said, “Good; I will make a covenant with you. But one thing I ask of you: you shall never appear in my presence unless you bring Saul’s daughter Michal when you come to see me.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Good! I, will solemnise with thee a covenant,—but, one thing, must I ask of thee, saying—Thou shalt not see my face, except thou have brought in Michal, Saul’s daughter, when thou comest to see my face.

text.NRSV: Then David sent messengers to Saul’s son Ishbaal, saying, “Give me my wife Michal, to whom I became engaged at the price of one hundred foreskins of the Philistines.”
text.Rotherham: And David sent messengers unto Ish-bosheth son of Saul, saying,—Give up my wife, Michal, whom I espoused to myself, for a hundred foreskins of Philistines.

text.NRSV: Ishbaal sent and took her from her husband Paltiel son of Laish.
text.Rotherham: So Ish-bosheth sent and took her from her husband,—from Paltiel, son of Laish.

text.NRSV: But her husband went with her, weeping as he walked behind her all the way to Bahurim. Then Abner said to him, “Go back home!” So he went back.
text.Rotherham: And her husband went along with her, weeping as he went behind her, as far as Behurim,—when Abner said unto him—Go, return. And he returned.

text.NRSV: Abner had a consultation with the elders of Israel, saying, “For some time past you have been seeking David as king over you.
text.Rotherham: Then had Abner, speech, with the elders of Israel, saying,—In times past, ye were seeking David, to be king over you.

text.NRSV: Now then bring it about; for the Lord has promised David: Through my servant David I will save my people Israel from the hand of the Philistines, and from all their enemies.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, act,—for Yahweh, spake unto David, saying, By the hand of David my servant, will I save my people Israel, out of the hand of the Philistines, and out of the hand of all their enemies.

text.NRSV: Abner also spoke directly to the Benjaminites; then Abner went to tell David at Hebron all that Israel and the whole house of Benjamin were ready to do.
text.Rotherham: And Abner also spake in the ears of Benjamin, —and Abner also went to speak in the ears of David, in Hebron, all that was pleasing in the eyes of Israel, and in the eyes of all the house of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: When Abner came with twenty men to David at Hebron, David made a feast for Abner and the men who were with him.
text.Rotherham: So, when Abner came in unto David, in Hebron, and, with him, twenty men, David made, for Abner, and for the men who were with him, a banquet.

text.NRSV: Abner said to David, “Let me go and rally all Israel to my lord the king, in order that they may make a covenant with you, and that you may reign over all that your heart desires.” So David dismissed Abner, and he went away in peace.
text.Rotherham: Then said Abner unto David—I will verily arise and go, and gather together unto my lord the king, all Israel—that they may solemnise with thee a covenant, so shalt thou reign over all that thy soul desireth. And David let Abner go, and he departed in peace.

text.NRSV: Just then the servants of David arrived with Joab from a raid, bringing much spoil with them. But Abner was not with David at Hebron, for David had dismissed him, and he had gone away in peace.
text.Rotherham: And lo! the servants of David, and Joab, came in from a raid, and, great spoil, brought they in with them; but, Abner, was not with David in Hebron, for he had let him go, and he had departed in peace.

text.NRSV: When Joab and all the army that was with him came, it was told Joab, “Abner son of Ner came to the king, and he has dismissed him, and he has gone away in peace.”
text.Rotherham: When, Joab, and all the host that was with him, had come in, then told they Joab, saying, Abner son of Ner hath been in unto the king, and he hath let him go, and he hath departed in peace.

text.NRSV: Then Joab went to the king and said, “What have you done? Abner came to you; why did you dismiss him, so that he got away?
text.Rotherham: Then Joab came in unto the king, and said—What hast thou done? Lo! Abner came in unto thee. Wherefore is it that thou didst let him go, so that he is clean departed?

text.NRSV: You know that Abner son of Ner came to deceive you, and to learn your comings and goings and to learn all that you are doing.”
text.Rotherham: Thou knowest Abner son of Ner, that, to deceive thee, he came,—and to take knowledge of thy going out, and thy coming in, and to take knowledge of all that thou art doing.

text.NRSV: When Joab came out from David’s presence, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the cistern of Sirah; but David did not know about it.
text.Rotherham: And, when Joab had come out from David, he sent messengers after Abner, and brought him back from the well of Sirah,—but, David, knew it not.

text.NRSV: When Abner returned to Hebron, Joab took him aside in the gateway to speak with him privately, and there he stabbed him in the stomach. So he died for shedding the blood of Asahel, Joab’s brother.
text.Rotherham: So Abner returned to Hebron, and Joab took him aside into the midst of the gate, to speak with him quietly,—and then smote him there, in the belly, that he died, for the blood of Asahel the brother of Joab.

text.NRSV: Afterward, when David heard of it, he said, “I and my kingdom are forever guiltless before the Lord for the blood of Abner son of Ner.
text.Rotherham: And, when David heard of it, after that, he said—Guiltless, am I and my kingdom before Yahweh, unto times age-abiding,—of the blood of Abner son of Ner.

text.NRSV: May the guilt fall on the head of Joab, and on all his father’s house; and may the house of Joab never be without one who has a discharge, or who is leprous, or who holds a spindle, or who falls by the sword, or who lacks food!”
text.Rotherham: Let it be hurled upon the head of Joab, and against all his father’s house,—and let there not fail from the house of Joab—one that hath an issue, or a leper, or one that leaneth on a crutch, or that falleth by the sword, or that lacketh bread.

text.NRSV: So Joab and his brother Abishai murdered Abner because he had killed their brother Asahel in the battle at Gibeon.
text.Rotherham: Thus, Joab and Abishai his brother, slew Abner,—because he had caused the death of Asahel their brother, in Gibeon, in the battle.

text.NRSV: Then David said to Joab and to all the people who were with him, “Tear your clothes, and put on sackcloth, and mourn over Abner.” And King David followed the bier.
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Joab, and unto all the people who were with him,—Rend your clothes, and gird you with sackcloth, and lament aloud before Abner. And, King David himself, was following the bier.

text.NRSV: They buried Abner at Hebron. The king lifted up his voice and wept at the grave of Abner, and all the people wept.
text.Rotherham: And they buried Abner in Hebron,—and the king lifted up his voice and wept, at the grave of Abner, and all the people wept.

text.NRSV: The king lamented for Abner, saying, “Should Abner die as a fool dies?
text.Rotherham: And the king addressed his dirge unto Abner, and said,—As a base man dieth, should Abner have died?

text.NRSV: Your hands were not bound, your feet were not fettered; as one falls before the wicked you have fallen.” And all the people wept over him again.
text.Rotherham: Thy hands, were not bound, And, thy feet, near to fetters, were not brought, As one falleth before assassins, so didst thou fall! And again all the people wept over him.

text.NRSV: Then all the people came to persuade David to eat something while it was still day; but David swore, saying, “God do so to me and more also, if I taste bread or anything else before the sun goes down!”
text.Rotherham: And, when all the people came near to get David to eat bread, while yet it was day, David sware, saying—So, let God do to me, and, so, let him add, if, before the sun go in, I taste bread, or anything else.

text.NRSV: All the people took notice of it, and it pleased them; just as everything the king did pleased all the people.
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, took note of it, and it was noble in their eyes,—as, all that the king did, was, in the eyes of all the people, noble.

text.NRSV: So all the people and all Israel understood that day that the king had no part in the killing of Abner son of Ner.
text.Rotherham: So then all the people, and all Israel, took note, that day, that it was not of the king, to cause the death of Abner son of Ner.

text.NRSV: And the king said to his servants, “Do you not know that a prince and a great man has fallen this day in Israel?
text.Rotherham: And the king said unto his servants,—Will ye not take note that, a prince and a great man, hath fallen this day, in Israel?

text.NRSV: Today I am powerless, even though anointed king; these men, the sons of Zeruiah, are too violent for me. The Lord pay back the one who does wickedly in accordance with his wickedness!”
text.Rotherham: And, I myself, this day, am weak, though anointed king, but, these men, the sons of Zeruiah, are more severe than I: Yahweh repay, the doer of wickedness, according to his wickedness.

text.NRSV: When Saul’s son Ishbaal heard that Abner had died at Hebron, his courage failed, and all Israel was dismayed.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Ish-bosheth son of Saul heard that Abner had died in Hebron, his hands became feeble,—and, all Israel, were dismayed.

text.NRSV: Saul’s son had two captains of raiding bands; the name of the one was Baanah, and the name of the other Rechab. They were sons of Rimmon a Benjaminite from Beeroth—for Beeroth is considered to belong to Benjamin.
text.Rotherham: And two men, captains of bands, pertained to Ish-bosheth son of Saul—the name of the one, was Baanah, and, the name of the other, Rechab—sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, of the sons of Benjamin,—for, even Beeroth, used to be reckoned unto Benjamin;

text.NRSV: (Now the people of Beeroth had fled to Gittaim and are there as resident aliens to this day).
text.Rotherham: and the Beerothites fled to Gittaim,—and have been sojourners there, until this day.

text.NRSV: Saul’s son Jonathan had a son who was crippled in his feet. He was five years old when the news about Saul and Jonathan came from Jezreel. His nurse picked him up and fled; and, in her haste to flee, it happened that he fell and became lame. His name was Mephibosheth.
text.Rotherham: And, Jonathan, son of Saul, had a son, lame of his feet,—five years old, was he, when tidings came in of Saul and Jonathan from Jezreel, so his nurse took him up, and fled, and it came to pass, when she started up to flee, that he fell and was lamed, and, his name, was, Mephibosheth.

text.NRSV: Now the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rechab and Baanah, set out, and about the heat of the day they came to the house of Ishbaal, while he was taking his noonday rest.
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rechab and Baanah, took their journey and came in, about the heat of the day, unto the house of Ish-bosheth,—when, he, was lying on his noonday couch;

text.NRSV: They came inside the house as though to take wheat, and they stabbed him in the stomach; then Rechab and his brother Baanah escaped.
text.Rotherham: and, thither, entered they as fro as the middle of the house, to fetch wheat, and they smote him in the belly,—and, Rechab and Baanah his brother, escaped.

text.NRSV: Now when they came into the house, as he lay on his couch in his bedchamber, they killed him, cut off his head, and taking his head, they traveled by way of the Arabah all night long.
text.Rotherham: Thus they entered the house when, he, was lying on his bed, in his sleeping-chamber, and smote him, and slew him, and beheaded him,—and took his head, and journeyed by way of the waste plain all the night;

text.NRSV: They brought the head of Ishbaal to David at Hebron and said to the king, “Here is the head of Ishbaal, son of Saul, your enemy, who sought your life; the Lord has avenged my lord the king this day on Saul and on his offspring.”
text.Rotherham: and brought in the head of Ish-bosheth unto David, at Hebron, and said unto the king, Lo! the head of Ish-bosheth son of Saul, thine enemy, who sought thy life: so hath Yahweh given to my lord the king, avengement this day, on Saul and on his seed.

text.NRSV: David answered Rechab and his brother Baanah, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, “As the Lord lives, who has redeemed my life out of every adversity,
text.Rotherham: Then David responded to Rechab and Baanah his brother, sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, and said unto them,—By the life of Yahweh, who hath redeemed my soul out of all distress,

text.NRSV: when the one who told me, ‘See, Saul is dead,’ thought he was bringing good news, I seized him and killed him at Ziklag—this was the reward I gave him for his news.
text.Rotherham: when he that brought tidings to me, saying, Lo! Saul is dead, though, he, was as one that bringeth good tidings, in his own eyes, yet I seized him, and slew him in Ziklag,—which was how I gave him reward for his tidings:

text.NRSV: How much more then, when wicked men have killed a righteous man on his bed in his own house? And now shall I not require his blood at your hand, and destroy you from the earth?”
text.Rotherham: how much more, when, lawless men, have slain a righteous person, in his own house, upon his bed? …Now, therefore, must I not require his blood at your hands, and so consume you, out of the earth?

text.NRSV: So David commanded the young men, and they killed them; they cut off their hands and feet, and hung their bodies beside the pool at Hebron. But the head of Ishbaal they took and buried in the tomb of Abner at Hebron.
text.Rotherham: David therefore commanded the young men, and they slew them, and cut off their hands, and their feet, and hanged them up over the pool, in Hebron,—but, the head of Ish-bosheth, took they, and buried in the grave of Abner, in Hebron.

text.NRSV: Then all the tribes of Israel came to David at Hebron, and said, “Look, we are your bone and flesh.
text.Rotherham: Then came all the tribes of Israel unto David, in Hebron,—and spake, saying—Behold us! thy bone and thy flesh, we are.

text.NRSV: For some time, while Saul was king over us, it was you who led out Israel and brought it in. The Lord said to you: It is you who shall be shepherd of my people Israel, you who shall be ruler over Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Also, in time past, when Saul was king over us, thou, wast he that led out and brought in, Israel,—and Yahweh said to thee—Thou, shalt be shepherd unto my people, Israel, and, thou, shalt become leader over Israel.

text.NRSV: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron; and King David made a covenant with them at Hebron before the Lord, and they anointed David king over Israel.
text.Rotherham: So all the elders of Israel came unto the king, in Hebron, and King David solemnised to them a covenant in Hebron, before Yahweh, —and they anointed David to be king over Israel.

text.NRSV: David was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years.
text.Rotherham: Thirty years old, was David when he began to reign,—forty years, he reigned:

text.NRSV: At Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months; and at Jerusalem he reigned over all Israel and Judah thirty-three years.
text.Rotherham: in Hebron, reigned he over Judah, seven years, and six months,—and, in Jerusalem, reigned he thirty and three years, over all Israel and Judah.

text.NRSV: The king and his men marched to Jerusalem against the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land, who said to David, “You will not come in here, even the blind and the lame will turn you back”—thinking, “David cannot come in here.”
text.Rotherham: Then went the king and his men, to Jerusalem, against the Jebusites, inhabiting the land,—and they spake to David, saying—Thou canst not come in hither, unless thou take away the blind and lame—Thinking, David will not come in hither.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless David took the stronghold of Zion, which is now the city of David.
text.Rotherham: So then David captured the citadel of Zion,—the same, is the city of David.

text.NRSV: David had said on that day, “Whoever wishes to strike down the Jebusites, let him get up the water shaft to attack the lame and the blind, those whom David hates.” Therefore it is said, “The blind and the lame shall not come into the house.”
text.Rotherham: And David said, on that day—Whosoever is smiting the Jebusites, then let him reach as far as the aqueduct. But, as for the lame and the blind, they were the hated of David’s soul,—for which cause, they kept on saying, Blind and lame! he will not enter the place.

text.NRSV: David occupied the stronghold, and named it the city of David. David built the city all around from the Millo inward.
text.Rotherham: So then David dwelt in the citadel, and called it, The City of David,—and David built round about, from Millo and inwards.

text.NRSV: And David became greater and greater, for the Lord, the God of hosts, was with him.
text.Rotherham: And David went on and on waxing great,—Yahweh, God of hosts, being with him.

text.NRSV: King Hiram of Tyre sent messengers to David, along with cedar trees, and carpenters and masons who built David a house.
text.Rotherham: Then Hiram, king of Tyre, sent messengers unto David, with cedar-wood, and carpenters, and masons, and they built a house for David.

text.NRSV: David then perceived that the Lord had established him king over Israel, and that he had exalted his kingdom for the sake of his people Israel.
text.Rotherham: So David perceived that Yahweh had established him to be king over Israel,—and that he had exalted his kingdom, for the sake of his people Israel.

text.NRSV: In Jerusalem, after he came from Hebron, David took more concubines and wives; and more sons and daughters were born to David.
text.Rotherham: And David took him yet more concubines and wives, out of Jerusalem, after he had come from Hebron, and there were, yet further, born unto David, sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: These are the names of those who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the names of the sons who were born unto him in Jerusalem,—Shammua and Shobab, and Nathan, and Solomon;

text.NRSV: Ibhar, Elishua, Nepheg, Japhia,
text.Rotherham: and Ibhar and Elishua and Nepheg and Yaphia;

text.NRSV: Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet.
text.Rotherham: and Elishama and Eliada, and Eliphalet.

text.NRSV: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over Israel, all the Philistines went up in search of David; but David heard of it and went down to the stronghold.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Philistine heard that they had anointed David to be king over Israel, then came up all the Philistines to seek to secure David, and David heard of it, and went down into the citadel.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines had come and spread out in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, had come,—and had spread themselves out in the vale of Rephaim.

text.NRSV: David inquired of the Lord, “Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you give them into my hand?” The Lord said to David, “Go up; for I will certainly give the Philistines into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: So then David enquired of Yahweh, saying,—Shall I go up against the Philistines? Wilt thou deliver them into my hand? And Yahweh said unto David—Go up; for I, will surely deliver, the Philistines into thy hand.

text.NRSV: So David came to Baal-perazim, and David defeated them there. He said, “The Lord has burst forth against my enemies before me, like a bursting flood.” Therefore that place is called Baal-perazim.
text.Rotherham: And David entered Baal-perazim, and David smote them there, and said—Yahweh hath broken forth upon mine enemies, before me, like a breaking forth of waters. For this cause, called he the name of that place, Baal-perazim.

text.NRSV: The Philistines abandoned their idols there, and David and his men carried them away.
text.Rotherham: And they left their images there,—and David and his men took them away.

text.NRSV: Once again the Philistines came up, and were spread out in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And, yet again, did the Philistines come up, —and they spread themselves out in the vale of Rephaim.

text.NRSV: When David inquired of the Lord, he said, “You shall not go up; go around to their rear, and come upon them opposite the balsam trees.
text.Rotherham: So David enquired of Yahweh, and he said—Thou shalt not go up,—Get round behind them, and come in upon them, over against the mulberry-trees.

text.NRSV: When you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then bestir yourself; for then the Lord has gone out before you to strike down the army of the Philistines.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when thou shalt hear a sound of marching in the tops of the mulberry-trees, then, shalt thou act with decision,—for, then, will Yahweh have gone forth before thee, to smite the host of the Philistines.

text.NRSV: David did just as the Lord had commanded him; and he struck down the Philistines from Geba all the way to Gezer.
text.Rotherham: And David did so, as Yahweh had commanded him,—and smote the Philistines, from Gibeon until thou enterest Gezer.

text.NRSV: David again gathered all the chosen men of Israel, thirty thousand.
text.Rotherham: And David, once more, gathered together all the choice young men in Israel, thirty thousand.

text.NRSV: David and all the people with him set out and went from Baale-judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, which is called by the name of the Lord of hosts who is enthroned on the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: Then David, and all the people that were with him, arose and went beyond Baale Judah,—to bring up from thence, the ark of God, the name whereof is called by the name of Yahweh of hosts, who inhabiteth the cherubim thereupon.

text.NRSV: They carried the ark of God on a new cart, and brought it out of the house of Abinadab, which was on the hill. Uzzah and Ahio, the sons of Abinadab, were driving the new cart
text.Rotherham: So they carried the ark of God in a new waggon, and brought it out of the house of Abinadab, who was in Gibeah,—and, Uzza and Ahio, sons of Abinadab, were driving the new waggon.

text.NRSV: with the ark of God; and Ahio went in front of the ark.
text.Rotherham: So they brought it out of the house of Abinadab, which was in Gibeah, with the ark of God,—and, Ahio, was going before the ark.

text.NRSV: David and all the house of Israel were dancing before the Lord with all their might, with songs and lyres and harps and tambourines and castanets and cymbals.
text.Rotherham: And, David and all the house of Israel, were dancing for joy before Yahweh, with all boldness and with songs,—and with lyres, and with harps, and with timbrels, and with sistrums, and with cymbals.

text.NRSV: When they came to the threshing floor of Nacon, Uzzah reached out his hand to the ark of God and took hold of it, for the oxen shook it.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came as far as the threshing-floor of Nachon, Uzzah thrust forth his hand unto the ark of God, and took hold of it, for the oxen were restive.

text.NRSV: The anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzzah; and God struck him there because he reached out his hand to the ark; and he died there beside the ark of God.
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Yahweh, against Uzzah, and God smote him there, for the error,—so that he died there, by the ark of God.

text.NRSV: David was angry because the Lord had burst forth with an outburst upon Uzzah; so that place is called Perez-uzzah, to this day.
text.Rotherham: And it was a sadness unto David, for that Yahweh had broken in with a breach against Uzzah,—therefore called he that place, Perez-uzzah [as it is called] until this day.

text.NRSV: David was afraid of the Lord that day; he said, “How can the ark of the Lord come into my care?”
text.Rotherham: And David was afraid of Yahweh, on that day,—and said, How can the ark of Yahweh come unto me?

text.NRSV: So David was unwilling to take the ark of the Lord into his care in the city of David; instead David took it to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
text.Rotherham: So David would not remove unto him the ark of Yahweh, unto the city of David,—but David took it aside to the house of Obed-edom, the Gittite.

text.NRSV: The ark of the Lord remained in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite three months; and the Lord blessed Obed-edom and all his household.
text.Rotherham: And the ark of Yahweh abode in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite, three months,—and Yahweh blessed Obed -edom, and all his household.

text.NRSV: It was told King David, “The Lord has blessed the household of Obed-edom and all that belongs to him, because of the ark of God.” So David went and brought up the ark of God from the house of Obed-edom to the city of David with rejoicing;
text.Rotherham: And it was told King David, saying, Yahweh hath blessed the household of Obed-edom, and all that he hath, because of the ark of God. David therefore went and brought up the ark of God, out of the house of Obed-edom, unto the city of David, with rejoicing.

text.NRSV: and when those who bore the ark of the Lord had gone six paces, he sacrificed an ox and a fatling.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when they who bare the ark of Yahweh had stepped forward six paces, he sacrificed an ox and a heifer.

text.NRSV: David danced before the Lord with all his might; David was girded with a linen ephod.
text.Rotherham: And, David, was dancing with all boldness before Yahweh,—David, being girt with an ephod of linen.

text.NRSV: So David and all the house of Israel brought up the ark of the Lord with shouting, and with the sound of the trumpet.
text.Rotherham: So, David and all the house of Israel, were bringing up the ark of Yahweh,—with triumphant shoutings, and with the sound of a horn.

text.NRSV: As the ark of the Lord came into the city of David, Michal daughter of Saul looked out of the window, and saw King David leaping and dancing before the Lord; and she despised him in her heart.
text.Rotherham: Now it so came about that, when the ark of Yahweh entered the city of David, Michal Saul’s daughter, looked out through the window, and saw King David, leaping and dancing before Yahweh, and she despised him, in her heart.

text.NRSV: They brought in the ark of the Lord, and set it in its place, inside the tent that David had pitched for it; and David offered burnt offerings and offerings of well-being before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So they brought in the ark of Yahweh, and set it in its place, in the midst of the tent which David had stretched out for it,—and David caused to go up ascending-sacrifices before Yahweh, and peace-offerings.

text.NRSV: When David had finished offering the burnt offerings and the offerings of well-being, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord of hosts,
text.Rotherham: And, when David had finished offering up the ascending-sacrifice and the peace-offerings, he blessed the people in the name of Yahweh of hosts.

text.NRSV: and distributed food among all the people, the whole multitude of Israel, both men and women, to each a cake of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins. Then all the people went back to their homes.
text.Rotherham: And he apportioned to all the people, to all the multitude of Israel, both to men and to women—to every one, a loaf of bread, and a sweet drink, and a raisin-cake,—and all the people went their way, every one unto his own house;

text.NRSV: David returned to bless his household. But Michal the daughter of Saul came out to meet David, and said, “How the king of Israel honored himself today, uncovering himself today before the eyes of his servants’ maids, as any vulgar fellow might shamelessly uncover himself!”
text.Rotherham: and David returned, to bless his household. Then came forth Michal Saul’s daughter to meet David, and said—How honoured, to-day, was the king of Israel in disrobing himself to-day, in the sight of the handmaids of his servants, as one of the low people, might disrobe himself?

text.NRSV: David said to Michal, “It was before the Lord, who chose me in place of your father and all his household, to appoint me as prince over Israel, the people of the Lord, that I have danced before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Michal, Before Yahweh, I will dance,—blessed, be Yahweh, who made choice of me above thy father, and above all his house, putting me in charge as leader over the people of Yahweh, over Israel,—therefore will I dance before Yahweh;

text.NRSV: I will make myself yet more contemptible than this, and I will be abased in my own eyes; but by the maids of whom you have spoken, by them I shall be held in honor.”
text.Rotherham: and will make myself, yet more, lightly esteemed that this, and become lowly in mine own eyes,—nevertheless, with the handmaids of whom thou hast spoken, with them, shall I be honoured.

text.NRSV: And Michal the daughter of Saul had no child to the day of her death.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Michal Saul’s daughter, had no child,—unto the day of her death.

text.NRSV: Now when the king was settled in his house, and the Lord had given him rest from all his enemies around him,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the king had taken up his abode in his house,—and Yahweh had given him rest round about, from all his enemies,

text.NRSV: the king said to the prophet Nathan, “See now, I am living in a house of cedar, but the ark of God stays in a tent.”
text.Rotherham: that the king said unto Nathan the prophet, See, I pray thee—I, have my abode in a house, of cedar, but, the ark of God, abideth in, the midst, of curtains.

text.NRSV: Nathan said to the king, “Go, do all that you have in mind; for the Lord is with you.”
text.Rotherham: And Nathan said unto the king, All that is in thy heart, go—do,—for Yahweh is with thee.

text.NRSV: But that same night the word of the Lord came to Nathan:
text.Rotherham: But so it was, in that night, that the word of Yahweh came unto Nathan, saying:

text.NRSV: Go and tell my servant David: Thus says the Lord: Are you the one to build me a house to live in?
text.Rotherham: Go and say unto my servant—unto David: Thus, saith Yahweh,—Shalt, thou, build me a house, for me to dwell in;

text.NRSV: I have not lived in a house since the day I brought up the people of Israel from Egypt to this day, but I have been moving about in a tent and a tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: seeing that I have not dwelt in a house, since the day that I brought up the sons of Israel out of Egypt, even unto this day,—but have been wandering in a tent as my habitation?

text.NRSV: Wherever I have moved about among all the people of Israel, did I ever speak a word with any of the tribal leaders of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd my people Israel, saying, “Why have you not built me a house of cedar?”
text.Rotherham: Wheresoever I have wandered with any of the sons of Israel, spake I ever, a word, with any one of the tribes of Israel, whom I charged to shepherd my people Israel, saying,—Wherefore have ye not built me a house, of cedar?

text.NRSV: Now therefore thus you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from following the sheep to be prince over my people Israel;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, thus, shalt thou say unto my servant, unto David: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, I myself, took thee away from the pasture, from after the flock,—to become leader over my people, over Israel;

text.NRSV: and I have been with you wherever you went, and have cut off all your enemies from before you; and I will make for you a great name, like the name of the great ones of the earth.
text.Rotherham: and was with thee, whithersoever thou didst go, and have cut off all thine enemies, from before thee,—and will make thee a name, like the name of the great ones who are in the earth;

text.NRSV: And I will appoint a place for my people Israel and will plant them, so that they may live in their own place, and be disturbed no more; and evildoers shall afflict them no more, as formerly,
text.Rotherham: and will appoint a place for my people, for Israel, and will plant them, and they shall inhabit their place, and be unsettled no more,—neither shall the sons of perversity again humiliate them, as at first;

text.NRSV: from the time that I appointed judges over my people Israel; and I will give you rest from all your enemies. Moreover the Lord declares to you that the Lord will make you a house.
text.Rotherham: even from the day when I put judges in charge over my people Israel, thus will I give thee rest from all thine enemies. And Yahweh must tell thee that, a house, will Yahweh make for thee.

text.NRSV: When your days are fulfilled and you lie down with your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, who shall come forth from your body, and I will establish his kingdom.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be that, when thy days shall be fulfilled, and thou shalt sleep with thy fathers, then will I raise up thy seed after thee, which proceedeth from thine own body,—and I will establish his kingdom.

text.NRSV: He shall build a house for my name, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom forever.
text.Rotherham: He, shall build a house for my name,—and I will establish his kingly throne unto times age-abiding:

text.NRSV: I will be a father to him, and he shall be a son to me. When he commits iniquity, I will punish him with a rod such as mortals use, with blows inflicted by human beings.
text.Rotherham: I, will become his father, And, he, shall become my son: If he commit iniquity, then will I correct him, with the rod of men, and with the stripes of the sons of men;

text.NRSV: But I will not take my steadfast love from him, as I took it from Saul, whom I put away from before you.
text.Rotherham: But, my lovingkindness, shall not depart from him,—as I caused it to depart from Saul, whom I caused to depart from before thee.

text.NRSV: Your house and your kingdom shall be made sure forever before me; your throne shall be established forever.
text.Rotherham: So shall thy house and thy kingdom be made steadfast unto times age-abiding, before thee,—thy throne, shall be established unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: In accordance with all these words and with all this vision, Nathan spoke to David.
text.Rotherham: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so, spake Nathan unto David.

text.NRSV: Then King David went in and sat before the Lord, and said, “Who am I, O Lord God, and what is my house, that you have brought me thus far?
text.Rotherham: Then entered King David, and tarried before Yahweh,—and said—Who am, I, My Lord, Yahweh, and what is my house, that thou hast brought me, hitherto;

text.NRSV: And yet this was a small thing in your sight, O Lord God; you have spoken also of your servant’s house for a great while to come. May this be instruction for the people, O Lord God!
text.Rotherham: and hast yet further made this seem little in thine eyes, My Lord, Yahweh, in that thou hast spoken, even of the house, of thy servant, for a great while to come? This, then is the law of manhood, O My Lord, Yahweh!

text.NRSV: And what more can David say to you? For you know your servant, O Lord God!
text.Rotherham: What more, then, can David yet further speak unto thee,—seeing that, thou thyself knowest thy servant, O My Lord Yahweh?

text.NRSV: Because of your promise, and according to your own heart, you have wrought all this greatness, so that your servant knows it.
text.Rotherham: For the sake of thine own word, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this great thing,—making it known unto thy servant.

text.NRSV: Therefore you are great, O Lord God; for there is no one like you, and there is no God besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, hast thou magnified thyself, O Yahweh Elohim,—for there is none like unto, thee, yea there is no God besides thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

text.NRSV: Who is like your people, like Israel? Is there another nation on earth whose God went to redeem it as a people, and to make a name for himself, doing great and awesome things for them, by driving out before his people nations and their gods?
text.Rotherham: Who, then, is like thy people, like Israel, a nation alone in the earth? whom God went to redeem for himself as a people, so to make himself a name, and to do for you the great deed, fearful things also for thy land, to make way for thy people, whom thou hadst redeemed for thyself, out of Egypt, [dealing with] nations and their god’s;

text.NRSV: And you established your people Israel for yourself to be your people forever; and you, O Lord, became their God.
text.Rotherham: and hast established for thyself thy people Israel—for thyself as a people, unto times age-abiding,—thou thyself, also, O Yahweh, becoming their God.

text.NRSV: And now, O Lord God, as for the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house, confirm it forever; do as you have promised.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh Elohim, the word which thou hast spoken concerning thy servant, and concerning his house, confirm thou, unto times age-abiding,—and do, as thou hast spoken:

text.NRSV: Thus your name will be magnified forever in the saying, ‘The Lord of hosts is God over Israel’; and the house of your servant David will be established before you.
text.Rotherham: that thy name may be age-abidingly magnified, saying, Yahweh of hosts, is God over Israel, and so, the house of thy servant David, be established before thee.

text.NRSV: For you, O Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, have made this revelation to your servant, saying, ‘I will build you a house’; therefore your servant has found courage to pray this prayer to you.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, O Yahweh of hosts, God of Israel, hast unveiled the ear of thy servant, saying—A house, will I build for thee. For this cause, hath thy servant found in his heart, to pray unto thee, this prayer.

text.NRSV: And now, O Lord God, you are God, and your words are true, and you have promised this good thing to your servant;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O My Lord, Yahweh, thou, art God, and, thy words, shall prove true,—therefore hast thou spoken unto thy servant this goodness.

text.NRSV: now therefore may it please you to bless the house of your servant, so that it may continue forever before you; for you, O Lord God, have spoken, and with your blessing shall the house of your servant be blessed forever.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, be pleased to bless the house of thy servant, that it may continue age-abidingly before thee,—for, thou, O My Lord, Yahweh, hast spoken, therefore, with thine own blessing, shall the house of thy servant be age-abidingly blessed.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, David attacked the Philistines and subdued them; David took Metheg-ammah out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them,—and David took the bridle of the metropolis, out of the hand of the Philistines.

text.NRSV: He also defeated the Moabites and, making them lie down on the ground, measured them off with a cord; he measured two lengths of cord for those who were to be put to death, and one length for those who were to be spared. And the Moabites became servants to David and brought tribute.
text.Rotherham: And he smote Moab, and measured them with the line casting, them down to the ground, and be measured with two lines to put to death, and with one full line to keep alive,—so the Moabites became David’s, as servants, bringing gifts.

text.NRSV: David also struck down King Hadadezer son of Rehob of Zobah, as he went to restore his monument at the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: And David smote Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah,—when he went to lay his hand on the River Euphrates.

text.NRSV: David took from him one thousand seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left enough for a hundred chariots.
text.Rotherham: And David captured from him, a thousand and seven hundred horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen,—and David destroyed all the chariots, but reserved of them, a hundred chariots.

text.NRSV: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand men of the Arameans.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer, king of Zobah, David smote of the Syrians, twenty-two thousand men.

text.NRSV: Then David put garrisons among the Arameans of Damascus; and the Arameans became servants to David and brought tribute. The Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus, and the Syrians became David’s, as servants bringing gifts,—and so Yahweh gave victory unto David, whithersoever he went.

text.NRSV: David took the gold shields that were carried by the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And David took the shields of gold which had come to the servants of Hadadezer,—and brought them to Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: From Betah and from Berothai, towns of Hadadezer, King David took a great amount of bronze.
text.Rotherham: also, from Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, did King David take bronze, exceeding much.

text.NRSV: When King Toi of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of Hadadezer,
text.Rotherham: And, when Tou king of Hamath heard that David had smitten all the forces of Hadadezer,

text.NRSV: Toi sent his son Joram to King David, to greet him and to congratulate him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him. Now Hadadezer had often been at war with Toi. Joram brought with him articles of silver, gold, and bronze;
text.Rotherham: then Tou sent Hadoram his son unto King David, to ask after his welfare, and to bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer and had smitten him, for Hadadezer had had wars with Tou,—and, in his hand, were vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and vessels of bronze.

text.NRSV: these also King David dedicated to the Lord, together with the silver and gold that he dedicated from all the nations he subdued,
text.Rotherham: Them also, did King David hallow unto Yahweh,—with the silver and the gold which he had hallowed from all the nations which he had subdued:

text.NRSV: from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, Amalek, and from the spoil of King Hadadezer son of Rehob of Zobah.
text.Rotherham: from Syria, and from Moab, and from the sons of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek,—and from the spoil of Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah.

text.NRSV: David won a name for himself. When he returned, he slew eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
text.Rotherham: And David made a name, when he returned from his smiting of the Syrians in the valley of salt,—eighteen thousand.

text.NRSV: He put garrisons in Edom; throughout all Edom he put garrisons, and all the Edomites became David’s servants. And the Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: And he put, in Edom, garrisons, throughout all Edom, put be garrisons, and so it was that all Edom became servants unto David,—and Yahweh gave victory unto David, whithersoever he went.

text.NRSV: So David reigned over all Israel; and David administered justice and equity to all his people.
text.Rotherham: And David reigned over all Israel,—and so it was that David used to execute justice and righteousness for all his people;

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
text.Rotherham: and, Joab, son of Zeruiah, was over the army,—and, Jehoshaphat, son of Ahilud, was remembrancer.

text.NRSV: Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests; Seraiah was secretary;
text.Rotherham: And, Zadok, son of Ahitub, and Abimelech, son of Abiathar, were priests,—and, Seraiah, was scribe;

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were priests.
text.Rotherham: and, Benaiah, son of Jehoiadah, was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites,—and, the sons of David, became, chief rulers.

text.NRSV: David asked, “Is there still anyone left of the house of Saul to whom I may show kindness for Jonathan’s sake?”
text.Rotherham: And David said, Is there yet one left unto the house of Saul,—that I may show him lovingkindness, for the sake of, Jonathan?

text.NRSV: Now there was a servant of the house of Saul whose name was Ziba, and he was summoned to David. The king said to him, “Are you Ziba?” And he said, “At your service!”
text.Rotherham: Now, unto the house of Saul, belonged a servant, whose name, was Ziba, and, when they had called him unto David, the king said unto him—Art, thou, Ziba? And he said—Thy servant!

text.NRSV: The king said, “Is there anyone remaining of the house of Saul to whom I may show the kindness of God?” Ziba said to the king, “There remains a son of Jonathan; he is crippled in his feet.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king—Is there never a man remaining unto the house of Saul, that I may show him the lovingkindness of God? And Ziba said unto the king, There remaineth a son unto Jonathan, lame in his feet.

text.NRSV: The king said to him, “Where is he?” Ziba said to the king, “He is in the house of Machir son of Ammiel, at Lo-debar.”
text.Rotherham: And the king said to him, Where is he? And Ziba said unto the king, Lo! he, is in the house of Machir son of Ammiel, in Lo-debar.

text.NRSV: Then King David sent and brought him from the house of Machir son of Ammiel, at Lo-debar.
text.Rotherham: Then sent King David,—and fetched him out of the house of Machir son of Ammiel, from Lo-debar.

text.NRSV: Mephibosheth son of Jonathan son of Saul came to David, and fell on his face and did obeisance. David said, “Mephibosheth!” He answered, “Your servant!”
text.Rotherham: Now, when Mephibosheth, son of Jonathan, son of Saul, came in unto David, he fell on his face and did homage. And David said, Mephibosheth? And he said, Lo! thy servant.

text.NRSV: David said to him, “Do not be afraid, for I will show you kindness for the sake of your father Jonathan; I will restore to you all the land of your grandfather Saul; and you yourself shall eat at my table always.”
text.Rotherham: And David said to him—Do not fear, for I will, indeed shew, thee lovingkindness, for the sake of Jonathan thy father, and will restore unto thee all the land of Saul thy father,—but, thou thyself, shall eat bread at my table, continually.

text.NRSV: He did obeisance and said, “What is your servant, that you should look upon a dead dog such as I?”
text.Rotherham: And he did homage, and said—What is thy servant,—that thou hast turned towards such a dead dog as I?

text.NRSV: Then the king summoned Saul’s servant Ziba, and said to him, “All that belonged to Saul and to all his house I have given to your master’s grandson.
text.Rotherham: Then the king called for Ziba, Saul’s servant, and said unto him,—All that pertained unto Saul and unto all his house, have I given unto the son of thy lord:

text.NRSV: You and your sons and your servants shall farm the land for him, and shall bring in the produce, so that your master’s grandson may have food to eat; but your master’s grandson Mephibosheth shall always eat at my table.” Now Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.
text.Rotherham: therefore shalt thou till for him the ground, thou, and thy sons, and thy servants, and shalt bring in, so that thy lord’s son may have bread to eat, but, Mephibosheth, thy lord’s son, shall continually eat bread at my table. Now, Ziba, had fifteen sons, and twenty servants.

text.NRSV: Then Ziba said to the king, “According to all that my lord the king commands his servant, so your servant will do.” Mephibosheth ate at David’s table, like one of the king’s sons.
text.Rotherham: Then said Ziba unto the king, According to all that my lord the king shall command his servant, so, will thy servant do,—but, Mephibosheth, is to eat at the table of David, as one of the sons of the king.

text.NRSV: Mephibosheth had a young son whose name was Mica. And all who lived in Ziba’s house became Mephibosheth’s servants.
text.Rotherham: Now, Mephibosheth, had a little son, whose name, was Micha. And, all that dwelt in the house of Ziba, were servants unto Mephibosheth.

text.NRSV: Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, for he always ate at the king’s table. Now he was lame in both his feet.
text.Rotherham: So, Mephibosheth, dwelt in Jerusalem, for, at the table of the king, continually, had he to eat,—he being lame, in both his feet.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, the king of the Ammonites died, and his son Hanun succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that the king of the sons of Ammon died,—and that Hanun his son reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: David said, “I will deal loyally with Hanun son of Nahash, just as his father dealt loyally with me.” So David sent envoys to console him concerning his father. When David’s envoys came into the land of the Ammonites,
text.Rotherham: Then said David—I will shew lovingkindness unto Hanun son of Nahash, as his father shewed, unto me, lovingkindness. So David sent to comfort him, by the hand of his servants, as to his father,—and the servants of David came into the land of the sons of Ammon.

text.NRSV: the princes of the Ammonites said to their lord Hanun, “Do you really think that David is honoring your father just because he has sent messengers with condolences to you? Has not David sent his envoys to you to search the city, to spy it out, and to overthrow it?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the rulers of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun their lord—Is David honouring thy father, in thine eyes, that he hath sent unto thee comforters? Is it not, for the sake of exploring the city, and spying it out, and overthrowing it, that David hath sent his servants unto thee?

text.NRSV: So Hanun seized David’s envoys, shaved off half the beard of each, cut off their garments in the middle at their hips, and sent them away.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore Hanun took David’s servants, and shaved off half their beards, and cut off their upper garments in the middle, as far as their buttocks,—and let them go.

text.NRSV: When David was told, he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Remain at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”
text.Rotherham: And, when they told David, he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed,—and the king said—Tarry at Jericho, until your beards be grown, then shall ye return.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that they had become odious to David, the Ammonites sent and hired the Arameans of Beth-rehob and the Arameans of Zobah, twenty thousand foot soldiers, as well as the king of Maacah, one thousand men, and the men of Tob, twelve thousand men.
text.Rotherham: And, when the sons of Ammon saw that they had made themselves odious with David, the sons of Ammon sent and hired the Syrians of Beth-rehob, and the Syrians of Zoba—twenty thousand footmen, and of king Maacah—a thousand men, and men of Tob—twelve thousand men.

text.NRSV: When David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army with the warriors.
text.Rotherham: And, when David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army of heroes,

text.NRSV: The Ammonites came out and drew up in battle array at the entrance of the gate; but the Arameans of Zobah and of Rehob, and the men of Tob and Maacah, were by themselves in the open country.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ammon came out, and set in array for battle, at the entrance of the gate,—whereas, the Syrians of Zoba, and of Rehob, and the men of Tob and of Maacah, were by themselves, in the field.

text.NRSV: When Joab saw that the battle was set against him both in front and in the rear, he chose some of the picked men of Israel, and arrayed them against the Arameans;
text.Rotherham: And, when Joab saw that the front of the battle was towards him, before and behind, he chose out of all the chosen men of Israel, and set them in array against the Syrians;

text.NRSV: the rest of his men he put in the charge of Abishai his brother, and he arrayed them against the Ammonites.
text.Rotherham: while, the rest of the people, he delivered into the hand of Abishai his brother,—and set [them] in array against the sons of Ammon.

text.NRSV: He said, “If the Arameans are too strong for me, then you shall help me; but if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will come and help you.
text.Rotherham: And he said—If the Syrians be too strong for me, then shalt thou become my deliverance,—but, if, the sons of Ammon, be too strong for thee, then will I come with deliverance to thee.

text.NRSV: Be strong, and let us be courageous for the sake of our people, and for the cities of our God; and may the Lord do what seems good to him.”
text.Rotherham: Be strong, and let us put forth our strength, for the sake of our people, and for the sake of the cities of our God,—and, Yahweh, do what is good in his own eyes.

text.NRSV: So Joab and the people who were with him drew near to battle against the Arameans; and they fled before him.
text.Rotherham: And Joab drew nigh, and the people that were with him, to fight against the Syrians,—and they fled before him.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, and entered the city. Then Joab returned from fighting against the Ammonites, and came to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the sons of Ammon, saw that the Syrians had fled, then fled they before Abishai, and entered the city,—so Joab returned from the sons of Ammon, and entered Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: But when the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they gathered themselves together.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Syrians saw that they were defeated before Israel, they gathered themselves together;

text.NRSV: Hadadezer sent and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the Euphrates; and they came to Helam, with Shobach the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.
text.Rotherham: and Hadadezer sent and brought out the Syrians that were beyond the River, and they entered Helam,—Shobach the prince of the host of Hadadezer being before them.

text.NRSV: When it was told David, he gathered all Israel together, and crossed the Jordan, and came to Helam. The Arameans arrayed themselves against David and fought with him.
text.Rotherham: And, when it was told David, he gathered together all Israel, and passed over the Jordan, and entered Helam,—and the Syrians set themselves in array to meet David, and fought with him.

text.NRSV: The Arameans fled before Israel; and David killed of the Arameans seven hundred chariot teams, and forty thousand horsemen, and wounded Shobach the commander of their army, so that he died there.
text.Rotherham: Then fled the Syrians before Israel, and David slew of the Syrians seven hundred chariots, and forty thousand horsemen,—Shobach also, prince of their host, smote he, that he died, there.

text.NRSV: When all the kings who were servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with Israel, and became subject to them. So the Arameans were afraid to help the Ammonites any more.
text.Rotherham: And, when all the kings who were servants to Hadadezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, they made peace with Israel, and served them,—and the Syrians feared to give help any more unto the sons of Ammon.

text.NRSV: In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, David sent Joab with his officers and all Israel with him; they ravaged the Ammonites, and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the return of the year, at the time of the going forth of kings, that David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel, and they destroyed the sons of Ammon, and laid siege to Rabbah,—but, David, was remaining in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: It happened, late one afternoon, when David rose from his couch and was walking about on the roof of the king’s house, that he saw from the roof a woman bathing; the woman was very beautiful.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, at eventide, David arose from his couch, and walked to and fro on the roof of the king’s house, when, from the roof, he saw a woman bathing herself,—the woman being exceeding beautiful to look upon.

text.NRSV: David sent someone to inquire about the woman. It was reported, “This is Bathsheba daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite.”
text.Rotherham: And David sent and enquired after the woman,—and one said—Is not, this, Bath-sheba, daughter of Eliam, wife of Uriah the Hittite?

text.NRSV: So David sent messengers to get her, and she came to him, and he lay with her. (Now she was purifying herself after her period.) Then she returned to her house.
text.Rotherham: And David sent messengers, and fetched her, and she came in unto him, and he lay with her, she having purified herself from her uncleanness,—and she returned unto her own house.

text.NRSV: The woman conceived; and she sent and told David, “I am pregnant.”
text.Rotherham: And the woman, having conceived, sent and told David, and said, I am with child.

text.NRSV: So David sent word to Joab, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” And Joab sent Uriah to David.
